commands.description: Returns the number of milliseconds passed since the Arduino board began running the current program; essential for non-blocking timing without using delay().
Allows formatting output in different bases (Hexadecimal, Decimal, Binary) for easier debugging of register values and raw sensor data.
Configures a hardware interrupt to trigger a specific Interrupt Service Routine (ISR) on a state change (RISING, FALLING, CHANGE), allowing immediate response to external events.
Provides non-volatile memory storage to save configuration settings or state that persists even after the power is disconnected.
Utilizes AVR power-down modes to reduce current consumption significantly during idle periods, critical for battery-operated IoT devices.
